questions.question: The Berlin Conference of 1884-85 saw European powers divide Africa among themselves without African input. Which country hosted this conference?
questions.explanation: The Berlin Conference was hosted by Otto von Bismarck in Berlin, establishing rules for the European colonization of Africa. Fourteen European nations attended; no African representatives were present. The conference set the principle that claims required 'effective occupation,' triggering a rush to colonize the continent.
questions.memory_hook: Berlin Conference 1884: Bismarck's Germany. 14 European powers, zero Africans. Carved up a continent.

questions.question: What is the term for an element or compound that speeds up a chemical reaction without being consumed in the process?
questions.explanation: Catalysts lower the activation energy of a reaction, making it proceed faster without being used up. Enzymes are biological catalysts (proteins). Platinum, palladium, and rhodium are catalysts in automotive catalytic converters that reduce exhaust pollution. Industrial processes like the Haber process (making ammonia for fertilizer) use iron as a catalyst.
questions.memory_hook: Catalyst: speeds reaction without being consumed. Enzymes are biological catalysts.

questions.question: Which American sprinter set the 100m world record of 9.58 seconds at the 2009 World Championships in Berlin?
questions.explanation: Usain Bolt's 9.58 seconds in Berlin remains the world record. He also holds the 200m record (19.19s, also set in Berlin 2009). At his peak, Bolt's top speed was measured at 44.72 km/h. He retired in 2017 after winning 8 Olympic gold medals across 2008, 2012, and 2016.
questions.memory_hook: Bolt: 9.58s world record, Berlin 2009. 44.72 km/h top speed. 8 Olympic golds.

questions.question: Which 1984 Prince film and soundtrack album, featuring hits like 'When Doves Cry' and 'Let's Go Crazy,' became one of the best-selling soundtracks of all time?
questions.explanation: Purple Rain (1984) was both a film and soundtrack. The album sold over 25 million copies. It won the Academy Award for Best Original Song Score — one of only three films in history to win that category. Prince wrote, produced, and performed all the music and also starred in the film.
questions.memory_hook: Purple Rain 1984: Prince wrote, produced, starred, and performed everything. Oscar winner.

questions.question: The Panama Canal connects the Atlantic and Pacific oceans. How long does a typical ship transit take?
questions.explanation: A typical transit through the Panama Canal takes 8-10 hours. The canal is 77 km long and uses a system of three locks to raise and lower ships 26 meters. About 14,000 vessels use it annually, carrying 5% of world trade. Ships pay tolls based on tonnage — the largest can pay over $1 million per crossing.
questions.memory_hook: Panama Canal: 77 km, 3 locks, 8-10 hour transit. 14,000 ships/year. Up to $1M toll.

questions.question: The Battle of Hastings in 1066 resulted in which Norman duke conquering England and becoming its king?
questions.explanation: On October 14, 1066, William of Normandy defeated King Harold II at the Battle of Hastings — Harold was reportedly killed by an arrow to the eye (though this is disputed). William was crowned King of England on Christmas Day 1066. The Norman Conquest fundamentally changed English language, culture, and society, introducing French vocabulary.
questions.memory_hook: William the Conqueror: Battle of Hastings Oct 14, 1066. Crowned Christmas Day 1066.

questions.question: Which artist, real name Abel Tesfaye, released the Grammy-winning album 'After Hours' in 2020 and performed the Super Bowl LV halftime show?
questions.explanation: The Weeknd (born in Toronto, of Ethiopian descent) performed at Super Bowl LV in February 2021. He notably funded the halftime show himself ($7 million) after the NFL didn't provide additional budget. 'Blinding Lights' from After Hours became one of the longest-charting songs in Billboard Hot 100 history.
questions.memory_hook: The Weeknd: Super Bowl LV, paid $7M himself. 'Blinding Lights' one of longest-charting Billboard songs.

questions.question: What is the name of the condition where the body's immune system attacks its own cells, as seen in diseases like lupus and rheumatoid arthritis?
questions.explanation: In autoimmune disorders, the immune system mistakenly identifies the body's own tissues as foreign. Over 80 autoimmune diseases are known. Multiple sclerosis attacks myelin sheaths in the nervous system; Type 1 diabetes destroys insulin-producing cells; rheumatoid arthritis attacks joint linings. Women are significantly more likely to develop autoimmune conditions.
questions.memory_hook: Autoimmune: immune system attacks self. 80+ diseases. Women more commonly affected.

questions.question: Which landlocked country, entirely surrounded by South Africa (plus a small border with Mozambique), is one of the smallest countries in the world?
questions.explanation: Lesotho is one of only three countries completely surrounded by a single other country (the others are San Marino and Vatican City, both surrounded by Italy). Lesotho sits on a high plateau — its lowest point (1,400 m) is higher than most countries' highest points. It is known as 'the Kingdom in the Sky.'
questions.memory_hook: Lesotho: entirely inside South Africa. 'Kingdom in the Sky.' Lowest point higher than most countries' peaks.

questions.question: The Ottoman Empire, at its peak in the 16th century under Suleiman the Magnificent, stretched from North Africa to Persia. Its capital was which city?
questions.explanation: Constantinople fell to the Ottomans under Mehmet II in 1453, ending the Byzantine Empire. It remained the Ottoman capital for 470 years. Suleiman the Magnificent expanded the empire to its greatest extent, ruling from 1520-1566. The Republic of Turkey was established in 1923, with Ankara as the new capital.
questions.memory_hook: Ottoman capital: Constantinople (fell 1453). Ankara became Turkish capital in 1923.

questions.question: Which rapper, real name Marshall Mathers, became one of the best-selling artists of all time with albums including 'The Slim Shady LP,' 'The Marshall Mathers LP,' and 'The Eminem Show'?
questions.explanation: Eminem has sold over 220 million records worldwide. 'The Marshall Mathers LP' (2000) is the fastest-selling rap album in history, moving 1.76 million copies in its first week. Discovered and produced by Dr. Dre, Eminem became one of the few white artists to achieve credibility in hip-hop.
questions.memory_hook: Eminem: 220M records sold. MMLP (2000) = fastest-selling rap debut ever. Discovered by Dr. Dre.

questions.question: In association football (soccer), what is the maximum length in minutes of additional time typically added at the end of each half, excluding injury time?
options: There is no fixed maximum — referee decides
questions.answer: There is no fixed maximum — referee decides
questions.explanation: Stoppage time (also called injury time) is at the referee's discretion based on stoppages for injuries, substitutions, goal celebrations, and time-wasting. FIFA introduced Board Decision guidelines but no hard cap. At the 2022 World Cup, some halves saw 10+ minutes of stoppage time, sparking controversy about inconsistency.
questions.memory_hook: Stoppage time: no fixed maximum. Referee judges based on delays. 2022 WC showed huge inconsistency.
